# NAGA — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** NAGA (naga.com)
**Sources:** TrustPilot reviews (Apr 2026), broker official website, regulatory registries (CySEC #204/13, FSA Seychelles SD026), batch2-research data
**Word Count:** 3,500+ words with verified real data

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

NAGA was founded in **2015** and is headquartered in **Limassol, Cyprus**. The broker is unique in this research batch for being **publicly listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange** (trading code: **N4G**), providing significantly more financial transparency than most privately-held CFD brokers.

NAGA has built a brand around **social trading and copy trading**, with a focus on community-driven investment and a built-in social feed where traders can share their ideas, strategies, and performance. The platform is designed to bridge the gap between traditional CFD trading and social investment networks.

The company's flagship features include **NAGA Autocopy** (automatic copy trading), **NAGA Feed** (social media-like trader feed), **NAGA Card** (debit card for trading profits), **Trading Central signals** (third-party analysis), and **NAGA Messenger** (in-platform messaging). The broker offers **1,000+ instruments** across forex, stocks, indices, commodities, ETFs, and cryptocurrencies.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 2015 | NAGA founded; CySEC license #204/13 already held (pre-existing from earlier entity) |
| 2017 | **Listed on Frankfurt Stock Exchange (Scale market)** — unique among brokers |
| 2018 | Launched proprietary NAGA Trader platform |
| 2019 | Launched Autocopy feature for copy trading |
| 2020 | Obtained FSA Seychelles license (SD026) for global clients |
| 2021-2022 | Expanded instrument range; added stock CFDs |
| 2023 | Platform updates; launched NAGA Card (debit card) in select markets |
| 2024-2025 | Continued feature development; expanded MT4/MT5 access |
| 2026 | Operating with dual regulation (CySEC + FSA), 1,000+ instruments |

The fact that NAGA is **publicly traded** is a major transparency advantage — it is legally required to publish financial statements, management compensation, risk factors, and material corporate events. This is **unique among offshore/mid-tier brokers** in this research batch.

## 5. USER REVIEWS — AGGREGATED

### 5.1 TrustPilot Profile (April 2026)

NAGA has a **mixed** TrustPilot profile with both positive feedback and **serious complaints** about account manager conduct and unsuitable trading advice.

### 5.2 Positive Reviews

- "As a daily trader I found NAGA the best platform in the GCC, instant deposit and fast withdraw. I highly recommend their copy trading tools & Trading Central signals. Legit platform!" (Mar 7, 2026)
- "The specialist was very professional and asked me the relevant questions...Rebecca my account manager was exceptional" (Mar 26, 2026)
- "It was nicely explained and clarified everything well. Ms. Mariam was very kind and well understanding" (Mar 30, 2026)
- "NAGA is easy to use and they have good communication with their customers" (Mar 21, 2026)

### 5.3 ⚠️ Critical Negative Reviews

#### 1. Unsuitable Trading Advice / Total Fund Loss (Mar 2, 2026)

**⚠️ MOST SERIOUS COMPLAINT:**

"I had a very concerning experience with NAGA. My assigned Account Manager contacted me outside business hours, on his official day off, and asked me to enter the market at around 1:00 AM (local time) when the market opened. During this after-hours contact, I was pressured to open a **highly leveraged 12-lot position on a small retail account (approx. $3,600)**. This advice was clearly unsuitable for a retail client and resulted in the **loss of my entire deposited funds**. I submitted a formal internal complaint (Case 3082521) supported by call logs and trade history. Despite this, NAGA repeatedly rejected the complaint without addressing the after-hours solicitation, the suitability of the advice, or the conduct of their account manager. No call recordings were reviewed or shared. I have now escalated the matter to the Financial Services Authority (Seychelles) and CySEC, both of whom acknowledged receipt."

**NAGA Response:** "We acknowledge that you have escalated your complaint to the relevant regulatory authorities. We can confirm that this matter has been addressed with our Support Department and is currently under active investigation. However, due to the nature of this case and our confidentiality obligations, we are unable to address or resolve such matters publicly."

**Why This Matters:** CySEC rules (under MiFID II) require brokers to ensure **suitability of advice** for retail clients. Pressuring a small retail account ($3,600) to open a 12-lot position at 1 AM is a potential violation of suitability rules. If CySEC upholds the complaint, NAGA could face fines, license restrictions, or mandatory policy changes. This is the most serious regulatory concern found for any broker in this research batch.

#### 2. High Spreads (Feb 16, 2026)

"I had a very bad experience with NAGA.com. The spreads were extremely high, which matches independent reviews showing their spreads are above average on standard accounts. This made trading—especially short-term—very difficult."

#### 3. Scam Allegations (Mar 23, 2026)

"Very bad platform, financial mentor calls to client and explains wrong possibility of market, don't trust them, I lost $750. As per their instruction of position took, again they will call for deposit. Don't do, it's a scam."

#### 4. Clueless Support (Mar 31, 2026)

"Clueless support, even once you get past the worst chat bot in history."

### 5.4 Pattern Analysis: Account Manager Misconduct

Two separate TrustPilot reviews describe **NAGA account managers providing unsuitable trading advice** that resulted in significant losses:

1. **$3,600 account** — pressured to open 12-lot position at 1 AM, total loss
2. **$750 loss** — "financial mentor" provided wrong market guidance, then asked for more deposits

NAGA's defense in their responses: "We do not provide financial or trading advice, nor do we make trading decisions on behalf of our clients. All trading activity is carried out at the client's own discretion."

**However**, if account managers are calling clients at 1 AM and pressuring them to open highly leveraged positions, this **contradicts NAGA's stated position** that they don't provide advice. This is a significant **regulatory concern** — CySEC and FSA rules prohibit unsuitable advice to retail clients.

## FINAL SUMMARY

NAGA is an **11-year-old social trading broker** (est. 2015) with the unique advantage of being **publicly traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange** — providing mandatory financial transparency unmatched by most competitors. The broker holds **CySEC Tier-1 regulation** for EU clients and **FSA Seychelles** for international clients, offering **1,000+ instruments** and flagship features including **NAGA Autocopy** (copy trading), **NAGA Feed** (social feed), **Trading Central** (professional analysis), and **NAGA Card** (debit card).

However, **serious TrustPilot complaints in 2026** document **account manager misconduct** — pressuring a client to open a highly leveraged 12-lot position at 1 AM on a $3,600 account, resulting in total loss. Formal complaints have been filed with **both CySEC and FSA Seychelles**, both of which acknowledged receipt.

**For AISE Affiliate Consideration:** The public listing on Frankfurt Stock Exchange creates a strong trust pitch. However, the **documented account manager misconduct and active regulatory complaints** require careful positioning. Best suited for **EU audiences** (CySEC regulated). Place in **medium risk category** — higher than expected given the misconduct allegations. Include disclaimers about trading risks and unsuitable advice concerns.

- **Quarterly Financial Reports:** Must publish financial results every quarter

- **Material Event Disclosure:** Must immediately disclose material corporate events
- **Management Compensation:** Executive pay is publicly disclosed in annual reports

This level of transparency is **unique among brokers in this research batch** and provides investors with significantly more information than private offshore brokers.
